# Bali city guide

## Overview

Bali is not one place but a whole island of contrasts: spiritual temples draped in offerings, terraced rice paddies glowing green, volcanic peaks above the clouds, and beach clubs bumping until dawn. Whether you want yoga and wellness or surf and nightlife, Bali delivers. Each region has its own personality.

**Best time to visit**: Apr-Oct (dry season). Jul-Aug is peak tourist season. Nov-Mar is wet but lush and cheaper.
**Currency**: Indonesian Rupiah (IDR). 15,700 IDR ≈ 1 USD.
**Language tip**: Bahasa Indonesia is the national language. English is widely spoken in tourist areas. "Terima kasih" (thank you) is essential.

## Neighborhoods

| Neighborhood | Vibe | Best for | Transit |
|---|---|---|---|
| Seminyak | Trendy, upscale | Beach clubs, fine dining, boutique shopping | Taxi/Grab from airport (30 min) |
| Ubud | Spiritual, lush | Rice terraces, yoga, art galleries, temples | Taxi/Grab from Seminyak (1.5 hours) |
| Canggu | Surf, digital nomad | Co-working, cafes, beach bars, surfing | Scooter/Grab from Seminyak (20 min) |
| Kuta/Legian | Budget, party | Nightclubs, surf lessons, budget shopping | Walk from airport (10 min) |
| Uluwatu | Clifftop, rugged | Surf breaks, temple, sunset views | Taxi/Grab from Seminyak (45 min) |
| Nusa Dua | Resort, calm | Luxury resorts, water sports, clean beaches | Taxi/Grab from airport (20 min) |
| Amed | Quiet, snorkeling | Diving, Mount Agung views, black sand beach | Drive from Ubud (2.5 hours) |
| Sanur | Relaxed, family | Sunrise beach, cycling, day trips to Nusa islands | Taxi/Grab from airport (25 min) |
| Ungasan | Clifftop, hidden coves | Karma Beach, Sundays Beach Club, luxury villas above the surf | Taxi/Grab from airport (40 min) |
| Nusa Lembongan | Slow island, snorkel | Devil's Tear, Dream Beach, mangroves, no real traffic | Fast boat from Sanur (30-40 min) |
| Gili Islands (Lombok) | Car-free escape | Trawangan (party + sunsets), Meno (quiet honeymoon), Air (best snorkeling) | Fast boat from Sanur, Padangbai or Lembongan (1.5-2 hrs) |

## Food

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description | Must-order |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Warung Babi Guling Ibu Oka | Balinese | Ubud | $ | Anthony Bourdain made this roast pig spot famous. Crispy skin, spiced pork, served with rice and sambal. | Babi guling (roast suckling pig) special |
| Locavore | Fine dining | Ubud | $$$ | Two chefs, all local ingredients, zero pretension. One of Asia's best restaurants. Book well in advance. | Tasting menu with local wine pairing |
| Warung Mak Beng | Seafood | Sanur | $ | Legendary fried fish warung. No menu, just one dish: fried fish with rice, sambal, and soup. Perfection. | Fried fish set (the only option) |
| Crate Cafe | Brunch | Canggu | $$ | The digital nomad breakfast spot. Smoothie bowls, avocado toast, and laptops everywhere. | Smoothie bowl, eggs Benedict |
| Naughty Nuri's | BBQ | Ubud/Seminyak | $$ | Famous pork ribs and dirty martinis. The ribs are smoky, sticky, and fall off the bone. | Pork spare ribs, dirty martini |
| La Baracca | Italian | Canggu | $$ | Wood-fired pizza in the middle of rice fields. The setting is as good as the food. | Margherita pizza, truffle pasta |
| Merah Putih | Indonesian | Seminyak | $$-$$$ | Modern Indonesian cuisine in a stunning cathedral-like bamboo building. Elevated versions of classics. | Nasi goreng, rendang, gado-gado |
| Warung Murah | Balinese | Seminyak | $ | A hidden local warung behind the tourist strip. Authentic nasi campur for a fraction of restaurant prices. | Nasi campur, ayam betutu |
| Single Fin | Beach bar/food | Uluwatu | $$ | Clifftop bar overlooking the surf break. Sunday sessions are legendary. Food is solid, views are the star. | Fish tacos, cold Bintang, sunset views |
| Milk and Madu | Brunch | Canggu/Ubud | $$ | Excellent all-day breakfast with strong coffee. The ricotta hotcakes are a cult favorite. | Ricotta hotcakes, cold brew, big breakfast |
| The Lazy Cats Café | Vegetarian | Ubud | $$ | Bright veg-forward café on Hanoman with rotating curries and pretty bowls. Easy second-day Ubud lunch. | Sayur lodeh curry, fresh juice |

## Landmarks and activities

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Time needed | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Tegallalang Rice Terraces | Nature | Ubud | $ | 1-2 hours | Iconic stepped rice paddies with Instagram-famous palm swing. Go early morning to avoid crowds. |
| Uluwatu Temple (Pura Luhur Uluwatu) | Culture | Uluwatu | $ | 2 hours | Clifftop temple at sunset with Kecak fire dance performance. Watch for monkeys, they steal sunglasses. |
| Tirta Empul Temple | Culture | Tampaksiring | $ | 1-2 hours | Holy spring water temple for purification rituals. Visitors can participate in the cleansing ceremony. Bring a sarong. |
| Mount Batur Sunrise Trek | Adventure | Kintamani | $$ | 5 hours (2 AM start) | Hike an active volcano for sunrise above the clouds. Scrambled eggs cooked in volcanic steam at the top. |
| Sacred Monkey Forest | Nature | Ubud | $ | 1-2 hours | 700 monkeys in a misty forest with ancient temples. Keep belongings secured, the monkeys are bold. |
| Nusa Penida Day Trip | Adventure | Off Bali | $$-$$$ | Full day | Dramatic cliffs, Kelingking Beach (T-Rex shape), Angel's Billabong. Fast boat from Sanur. |
| Seminyak Beach Sunset | Beach | Seminyak | Free | 2 hours | Golden hour on the beach with beanbags, cold drinks, and sky on fire. Potato Head or Ku De Ta for sundowners. |
| Tanah Lot Temple | Culture | Tabanan | $ | 1-2 hours | Sea temple on a rock formation. Accessible at low tide. Stunning at sunset. 45 minutes from Seminyak. |
| Bali Swing | Adventure | Ubud | $$ | 1-2 hours | Swing over a jungle valley for the ultimate photo. Multiple locations around Ubud. |
| Sidemen Valley | Nature | East Bali | Free-$ | Half day | Peaceful alternative to Ubud. Rice terraces, Mount Agung views, traditional villages. |
| Kuta Beach | Beach | Kuta | Free | Half day | The classic Bali starter beach: long stretch of sand, beginner-friendly surf, sunset crowds with cold Bintangs. |
| Sundays Beach Club | Beach club | Ungasan | $$-$$$ | Half day | Hidden white-sand cove below the Ungasan cliffs (inclinator down). Snorkel gear, kayaks, beach bonfire at sunset. |
| Devil's Tear | Nature | Nusa Lembongan | Free | 1-2 hours | Lembongan's signature cliff blowhole: waves explode through the rocks just before sunset. Pair with Dream Beach next door. |
| Kelingking Beach | Beach | Nusa Penida | $ | 2-4 hours | The T-Rex shaped cliff: Bali's most-photographed coastline. The viewpoint is free; the brutal staircase down to the sand takes ~1 hour each way. |
| Gili Trawangan | Beach | Gili Islands, Lombok | $$ | 1-2 days | The biggest, busiest Gili. No cars, no motorbikes — just bikes and cidomo carts. Sunset on the west side, night market on the east. |
| Gili Meno | Beach | Gili Islands, Lombok | $ | Day trip | The quietest of the three Gilis. Salt lake in the middle, the famous underwater statues offshore, barely any nightlife. Honeymoon island. |
| Gili Meno Turtle Point & Statues | Snorkel | Gili Islands, Lombok | $ | 2-3 hours | Snorkel off Meno's west side to see green sea turtles and Jason deCaires Taylor's 'Nest' — a ring of 48 underwater human statues being colonized by coral. |
| Gili Air | Beach | Gili Islands, Lombok | $-$$ | 1-2 days | Goldilocks Gili: sleepy enough to feel remote, alive enough for beach bars and yoga. Best snorkeling straight off the east beach. |

## Nightlife

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Potato Head Beach Club | Beach club | Seminyak | $$-$$$ | The ultimate Bali beach club. Pool, restaurant, sunset views, DJ sets. Iconic for a reason. |
| Old Man's | Bar | Canggu | $-$$ | The surfer bar of Canggu. Live music, cheap beer, Wednesday trivia, Sunday sessions. |
| La Favela | Club | Seminyak | $$ | Jungle-themed club with multiple levels. Goes until late. Bohemian decor, strong drinks. |
| Omnia Dayclub | Club | Uluwatu | $$$ | Clifftop mega-club with infinity pool overlooking the ocean. International DJs, dramatic setting. |
| Jazz Cafe | Live music | Ubud | $$ | Live jazz and traditional Balinese music on alternating nights. Low-key, artistic, Ubud's evening scene. |

## Transit tips

- **No public transit**: Bali has no subway, train, or reliable bus system. Plan accordingly.
- **Grab**: The rideshare app works but is technically banned from some tourist areas. Use discreetly.
- **Scooter rental**: The local way to get around. About $5/day. International license or local permit required. Traffic is chaotic.
- **Private driver**: Hire a driver for the day ($35-50). Best for temple tours and cross-island trips. Ask your hotel.
- **Walking**: Only practical within neighborhoods like central Ubud or Seminyak beach strip.
- **Pro tip**: Traffic between Seminyak and Ubud can be 2+ hours during rush hour. Leave early.
- **Airport**: Ngurah Rai International Airport (DPS). Located in the south near Kuta. Prepay taxi from official counter.

## Useful Indonesian phrases

| English | Indonesian | Romanization |
|---|---|---|
| Hello | Halo | HA-lo |
| Thank you | Terima kasih | teh-REE-mah KAH-see |
| Yes/No | Ya/Tidak | yah / TEE-dahk |
| How much? | Berapa? | beh-RAH-pah |
| Delicious! | Enak! | EH-nahk |
| Where is...? | Di mana...? | dee MAH-nah |
| Beautiful | Bagus | BAH-goos |
| Thank you (Balinese) | Suksma | SOOK-smah |
| No thank you | Tidak, terima kasih | TEE-dahk teh-REE-mah KAH-see |
